返回本批次

显示 HN：带有 Vim 键绑定的终端电子表格编辑器

Show HN: A terminal spreadsheet editor with Vim keybindings

github.com 作者 garritfra 分数 93 评论 47 2026/04/27 19:39
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 带有 Vim 键绑定的终端电子表格编辑器，用 Rust 编写。
  2. 从 crates.io 安装：cargo install cell-sheet-tui 适用于 Linux、macOS 和 Windows 的预构建二进制文件可在 GitHub 版本页面上找到。从源代码构建 git clone https://github.com/garritfra/cell.git cd cell Cargo build --release # 目标/发布/单元中的二进制文件 使用 cell # 空工作表 cell data.csv # 打开 CSV 单元 data.tsv # 打开 TSV 单元sheet.cell # 打开本机格式 要探索包含公式、范围和 IF 逻辑的示例工作表：cell Examples/demo.cell 无头模式 对于 shell 管道、Makefile 和 CI，cell 可以在不启动 TUI 的情况下读取和写入文件：cell sales.cell --read A1 # 打印一个单元格的计算值 cell sales.cell --read B1:B10 # 将范围打印为 TSV cell sales.cell --eval '=SUM(B1:B10)' # 计算公式（不保存） cell sales.cell --write A1 42 # 设置一个单元格，重新计算，就地保存单元格 sales.cell --write A1 42 --write B1 7 # 将多个写入批量写入一个保存单元格 sales.cell --write Total '=SUM(B:B)' --read Total # 写一个公式，然后打印出来
  3. 单元格引用为 1 索引和 Excel 样式（A1、AA10、A1:B3）。 --eval 上的 = 前缀是可选的。值以 = 开头的写入存储为公式；其他是自动输入的（数字与文本）。每次调用都按固定顺序应用操作：写入 → 保存 → 读取 → 评估。错误打印到 stderr；如果引用错误、解析错误或丢失文件，进程会以非零值退出。
  4. 键绑定 如果您了解 Vim，您就了解 cell。普通模式
  5. 关键行动

Original Summary

  1. A terminal spreadsheet editor with Vim keybindings, written in Rust.
  2. Install From crates.io: cargo install cell-sheet-tui Pre-built binaries for Linux, macOS, and Windows are available on the GitHub Releases page. Build from source git clone https://github.com/garritfra/cell.git cd cell cargo build --release # Binary at target/release/cell Usage cell # empty sheet cell data.csv # open CSV cell data.tsv # open TSV cell sheet.cell # open native format To explore an example sheet with formulas, ranges, and IF logic: cell examples/demo.cell Headless mode For shell pipelines, Makefiles, and CI, cell can read from and write to a file without launching the TUI: cell sales.cell --read A1 # print one cell's computed value cell sales.cell --read B1:B10 # print a range as TSV cell sales.cell --eval '=SUM(B1:B10)' # evaluate a formula (no save) cell sales.cell --write A1 42 # set a cell, recalc, save in place cell sales.cell --write A1 42 --write B1 7 # batch multiple writes into one save cell sales.cell --write Total '=SUM(B:B)' --read Total # write a formula, then print it
  3. Cell references are 1-indexed and Excel-style (A1, AA10, A1:B3). The = prefix on --eval is optional. Writes whose value starts with = are stored as formulas; others are auto-typed (number vs text). Operations apply in a fixed order per invocation: writes → save → reads → evals. Errors print to stderr; the process exits non-zero on bad refs, parse errors, or missing files.
  4. Keybindings If you know Vim, you know cell. Normal Mode
  5. Key Action

评论（默认中文）

freedomben 2026/04/28 01:02
非常好，急需这样的工具。 看到它生锈了也很酷（这意味着我可以破解它）。

不过，要对我真正有用（我会想象其他人），它至少需要能够 看法 （但可能编辑）xlsx 和/或开放文档格式电子表格。 我知道这是一项艰巨的任务，但它将为该项目增加巨大的价值。 我不是在要求它，我也不觉得“有权”这样做（以免不可避免地收到“自己添加”的回复），只是试图提供一些有用的反馈。

问题：您认为这是一个长期致力于的项目吗？ （显然生活中的事件会发生并且事情会发生变化，但至少现在，您喜欢它吗？您是否将其用作工作流程的一部分？您是否觉得这是您想要长期拥有的东西？）

Very neat, a tool like this is badly needed. Also cool to see it's in rust (that means I can hack on it).

To be really useful to me though (and I would imagine others), it will need to be able to at least view (but probably edit) xlsx and/or open document format spreadsheets. I know that's a huge task, but it would add immense value to the project. I'm not asking for it and I don't feel "entitled" to it (lest the inevitable replies of "add it youself" come flying in), just trying to provide some helpful feedback.

Question: is this a project you see yourself staying committed to long term? (Obviously life events happen and things change, but at least for now, do you enjoy it? Do you use it as part of your workflow? Do you feel like it's something you'll want to own long term?)

在 HN 中查看
garritfra 2026/04/28 03:55
谢谢你！

XLSX 和 ODS 支持并非微不足道，但我看到了拥有它的价值。我将其添加到路线图中： https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/22

关于你的问题：这个想法已经在我的待办事项列表 [1] 上存在了 3 年多了。我通常不会在日常工作中使用大型数据文件，但偶尔在 macOS 上使用奇怪的 CSV 会让我很沮丧，因此将其列入列表中。现在规范驱动的开发已经足够成熟，可以真正有用，没有真正的借口不构建它。

我真诚地认为自己会长期关心这个问题。我对这个范围感到满意，而且社区似乎确实对此感兴趣。

[1]： https://garrit.xyz/todo

Thank you!

XLSX and ODS support won't be trivial, but I see the value of having it. I added it to the roadmap: https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/22

Regarding your question: This idea has been sitting on my todo list [1] for over 3 years now. I don't usually work with large data files in my dayjob, but occasionally working with the odd CSV on macOS frustrated me enough to put it on the list. And now that spec-driven development has matured enough to be actually useful, there's no real excuse not to build this.

I genuinely see myself caring for this long-term. I'm comfortable with the scope, and there seems to be some real interest from the community.

[1]: https://garrit.xyz/todo

在 HN 中查看
rpassos 2026/04/27 22:51
我一直在使用 https://github.com/medialab/xan 对于任何 csv.它是读取和分析 csv 文件的最佳工具，但不是写入它们的最佳工具。像 cell-sheet-tui 这样的东西非常适合我的工作流程，因为我使用 neovim 手动编辑 csv 文件（以及 xan 提供的一些 csv 合并功能来简化流程）。

我之所以觉得你们的软件很有前途，是因为处理纯文本中的 csv 会因为水平单元格空间不一致而非常迷失方向。一个支持 csv 的文本编辑器将在解决两个问题上提供很大帮助：

- 不必手动设置逗号，尤其是不必在空单元格之间计算逗号

- 能够使用 h/j/k/l 在单元格之间导航，而不必在纯文本上使用 vim 动作发挥创意

OP，如果你正在读这篇文章：

如果这个工具能够专注于写作体验，而不是与任何公式引擎相关的东西，我将不胜感激，因为 xan 已经是一个非常高性能的工具，即使在大型数据集上也是如此。在我看来，取代 xan 甚至与 xan 竞争似乎非常困难，因为这两种工具都针对相似的受众（rust、极简主义和性能），这让我认为 cell 可以更多地关注写作体验，就像我说的那样。

我立即纠正了一些错误，并会在适当的时候提出问题。感谢您制作这个，我希望将来我可以用 cell 替换 neovim 来处理任何 csv！

I've been using https://github.com/medialab/xan for anything csv. It's the best tool there is for reading and analyzing csv files, but not for writing them. Something like cell-sheet-tui would fit my workflow very well, since I am using neovim to edit csv files manually (as well as some csv merging features offered by xan to simplify the process).

The reason why I find your software promising is because dealing with csv in plain text is very disorienting with the inconsistent horizontal cell space. A csv-aware text editor would help a lot there with two issues in particular:

- not having to set commas manually, especially not having to count them between empty cells

- being able to navigate using h/j/k/l between cells instead of having to get creative with vim-motions on plain text

OP, if you're reading this:

I would greatly appreciate if this tool could focus on the writing experience and less with anything formula engine related, since xan is already a very performant tool for that, even at large data sets. Replacing or even competing with xan seems very difficult in my eyes, considering both tools are targeting a similar audience (rust, minimalist, and performance), which makes me think cell could focus more on the writing experience, like I said.

There are some bugs I countered right away and I'll get to open issues in due time. Thanks for making this and I hope I can replace neovim with cell for anything csv in the future!

在 HN 中查看
garritfra 2026/04/27 23:10
作者在这里 - 感谢您提出这个问题！

Cell 非常适合您的需求。我的愿景是“Excel 但它是 (Neo)Vim”。编辑文件应该像查看数据一样顺利。我相信 Xan 和 Cell 实际上可以很好地相互连接以渲染更复杂的数据。

我非常感谢您花时间报告您遇到的错误。期待他们。

Author here - thanks for bringing this up!

Cell is very much tailored towards what you're looking for. My vision was "Excel but it's (Neo)Vim". Editing files should feel just as smooth as looking at the data. I believe Xan and Cell could actually pipe into each other quite nicely for rendering more complex data.

I'd really appreciate your time to report the bugs you encountered. Looking forward to them.

PaulBunch 2026/04/28 16:49
这真的很酷。单元对齐和整体可扫描性非常出色。

我在许多 TUI 工具中注意到的一件事是，即使是字符宽度或图标渲染方面的微小不一致也会很快变得烦人。您在构建 Ratatui 时是否遇到过任何棘手的渲染/对齐问题？

This is really cool. The cell alignment and overall scannability are excellent.

One thing I’ve noticed in many TUI tools is that even small inconsistencies in character width or icon rendering quickly become annoying. Did you run into any tricky rendering/alignment issues while building on ratatui?

在 HN 中查看
nanis 2026/04/28 04:05
这看起来不错，但是，我注意到：

> 支持的功能 (v1) > 求和、平均、计数、最小值、最大值、IF

我建议尽早改用数值稳定的算法来添加数字并取平均值。

查看代码[1]，看起来您只是简单地对数字求和并除以计数。如果确实如此，请切换到稳定的平均值[2]。另见[3]。

另外，我尝试在Windows上测试可执行文件[4]，但输入有问题。使用 Windows 终端版本：1.24.10921.0，输入时字符加倍。使用 Visual C++ 编译的常规 Vim 不存在此问题。

当我按 `:` 时，我会在命令行中看到 `::` 。然后，我按下的每个键都会加倍。这意味着我不能`:q!`。 :-)

[1]： https://github.com/garritfra/cell/blob/99b0afc7a60a871b27452...

[2]： https://www.nu42.com/2015/03/how-you-average-numbers.html

[3]： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algorithms_for_calculating_var...

[4]： https://github.com/garritfra/cell/releases/tag/v0.2.0

This looks good, but, I noticed:

> Supported Functions (v1) > SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MIN, MAX, IF

I would recommend switching to numerically stable algorithms for adding numbers and taking their averages sooner rather than later.

Looking at the code[1], it looks like you are simply summing the numbers and dividing by count. If that is indeed the case, please switch to a stable average[2]. See also [3].

Additionally, I tried to test the executable[4] on Windows, but there is something wrong with input. Using Windows Terminal Version: 1.24.10921.0, characters are doubled on input. Regular Vim compiled using Visual C++ does not have this issue.

When I press `:`, I get `::` in the COMMAND line. Then, every key I press gets doubled. This means I cannot `:q!`. :-)

[1]: https://github.com/garritfra/cell/blob/99b0afc7a60a871b27452...

[2]: https://www.nu42.com/2015/03/how-you-average-numbers.html

[3]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algorithms_for_calculating_var...

[4]: https://github.com/garritfra/cell/releases/tag/v0.2.0

在 HN 中查看
garritfra 2026/04/28 04:42
这是很有价值的反馈。我打开了两个问题来跟踪这个问题：

对 SUM 和 AVERAGE 使用数值稳定算法： https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/43

Windows 终端中的击键次数增加了一倍： https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/44

谢谢！

This is valuable feedback. I opened two issues to track this:

Use numerically stable algorithms for SUM and AVERAGE: https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/43

Keystrokes doubled in Windows Terminal: https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/44

Thanks!

Pretty cool project! Congrats.

QQ: does it support programmatic cell access/modifications?

Eg `cell file.cell --write A2 "42"` or `cell file.cell --read "=SUM(A1:A10)"`? Couldn't surmise that from the glimpse I gave the README, but it would be pretty cool for scripting stuff.

在 HN 中查看
SilentEditor 2026/04/27 20:08
很酷的项目！

Vim 模态模型感觉对于电子表格来说几乎是原生的，并且将公式引擎拆分为无 TUI 的核心可能是一个很好的架构选择。

随着工作表的增长，特别是当多单元格编辑和更大的 CSV 开始给核心带来压力时，您如何考虑重新计算、依赖性跟踪和撤消/重做？

Cool project!

The Vim modal model feels almsot native for spreadsheets, and splitting the formula engine into a TUI-free core was prolly a great architectural choice.

How are you thinking about recalculation, dependency tracking, and undo/redo as the sheet grows, especially once multi-cell edits and larger CSVs start stressing the core?

在 HN 中查看
garritfra 2026/04/27 22:07
谢谢！

老实说，当前的实现相当幼稚——它们通过了测试，并且在我使用的小床单上感觉很敏捷，但它们在实际负载下很快就会弯曲。您所询问的大部分内容已经在追踪器上；我以您的评论作为提示打开了一批问题。

重新计算。现在，每次编辑都会完全重新计算：重新计算收集所有公式单元格，计算整个公式集的度数，拓扑排序，并从上到下进行评估。 dirty 标志由 mark_dirty 传播，但实际上并不用于修剪工作。它还在每次传递时重新解析原始字符串中的每个公式。有两个问题涵盖了这一点：#8 引入了批处理边界，因此粘贴/填充/CSV 导入会触发一次重新计算而不是 N，而#7 添加了标准基准，因此我们可以在优化之前实际判断解析器、BFS 或拓扑排序是否是热点。一旦#7 确认解析占主导地位，Cell 上的 AST 缓存显然是下一步。

https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/8 https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/7

依赖性跟踪。更大的味道是在 extract_deps 中——像 SUM(A1:A1000) 这样的范围实际上将 1000 个单元格位置枚举到 dep 图中，每一侧的每个单元格都有一个 HashSet。数百个细胞还好，数十万个细胞则灾难。范围扩展是#7 中的基准案例之一；正确的修复（间隔键控的 deps，因此范围保持一流而不是散开）还没有自己的问题 - 我应该打开一个，因为 #7 只测量问题。

撤消/重做。这是目前最严重的罪犯。 UndoEntry 直到最近才出现单细胞变体； #12 添加了 MultiCellEdit，但 #13 跟踪了我错过的两条破坏性路径——视觉模式 d 和 p/P 粘贴——它们仍然根本不推送撤消条目。 #9 是更广泛的合并故事（一个 dd = 一个撤消，CSV 导入 = 一个撤消等），与 #8 中的批处理机制相关联，因此单个事务会生成单个撤消条目。 sort_by_column 现在也是不可撤消的，属于该存储桶。

https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/13 https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/9

更大的 CSV。存储是 HashMap<CellPos, Cell> — 精细稀疏，但每个单元都有开销；对于非常广泛的导入，面向列或竞技场的布局会得到回报。不过我还没有对其进行过分析，所以这是推测性的；依赖图爆炸会先于原始存储造成伤害。 #7 包括一个 100k 行 CSV 负载案例，用于在其上放置数字。

#10 是元问题，将所有这些内容从源代码注释中提取出来并放入实际的架构文档中，我可能应该在发布之前完成这些操作。

https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/10

所以：今天这里没有什么可扩展的，但架构划分得足够干净，以至于不需要重写——AST 缓存、脏集重新计算、范围感知依赖和分组撤消是四个线程，并且大多数都有附加问题。

Thanks!

Honestly, the current implementations are pretty naive — they pass the tests and feel snappy on the small sheets I work with, but they'd buckle pretty quickly under real load. Most of what you're asking about is already on the tracker; I opened a batch of issues citing your comment as the prompt.

Recalculation. Right now it's a full recalc on every edit: recalculate collects all formula cells, computes in-degrees across the whole formula set, topo-sorts, and evaluates top to bottom. The dirty flag gets propagated by mark_dirty but isn't actually used to prune work. It's also re-parsing every formula from its raw string on every pass. Two issues cover this: #8 introduces a batch boundary so paste/fill/CSV import trigger one recalc instead of N, and #7 adds criterion benches so we can actually tell whether the parser, the BFS, or the topo sort is the hotspot before optimizing. AST caching on Cell is the obvious next step once #7 confirms parsing dominates.

https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/8 https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/7

Dependency tracking. The bigger smell is in extract_deps — a range like SUM(A1:A1000) literally enumerates 1000 cell positions into the dep graph, with a HashSet per cell on each side. Fine at hundreds of cells, a disaster at hundreds of thousands. Range expansion is one of the bench cases in #7; the proper fix (interval-keyed deps so ranges stay first-class instead of fanning out) doesn't have its own issue yet — I should open one, since #7 only measures the problem.

Undo/redo. This is the worst offender right now. UndoEntry only had a single-cell variant until very recently; #12 added MultiCellEdit, but #13 tracks two destructive paths I missed — visual-mode d and p/P paste — that still don't push undo entries at all. #9 is the broader coalescing story (one dd = one undo, CSV import = one undo, etc.), tied to the batch mechanism from #8 so a single transaction produces a single undo entry. sort_by_column is also non-undoable today and belongs in that bucket.

https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/13 https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/9

Larger CSVs. Storage is HashMap<CellPos, Cell> — fine sparse but with overhead per cell; for very wide imports a column-oriented or arena layout would pay off. I haven't profiled it though, so this is speculative; the dependency-graph blowup will hurt before raw storage does. #7 includes a 100k-row CSV load case to put numbers on it.

And #10 is the meta-issue to lift all of this out of source comments and into actual architecture docs, which I probably should have done before posting.

https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/10

So: nothing here scales today, but the architecture splits cleanly enough that none of it needs a rewrite — AST caching, dirty-set recalc, range-aware deps, and grouped undo are the four threads, and most have issues attached.

在 HN 中查看
s_suiindik 2026/04/27 23:28
一流的航程是正确的优先事项。有效的模式：保留 范围作为单个 AST 节点（每个范围一个深度边，而不是 N），然后使用 间隔树位于反面，因此 C5 处的单元格更改变为 “查找覆盖 (C, 5) 的区间”而不是扫描所有公式。 如果您去那里，与面向列的存储完美搭配

在 AST 缓存点上，值得通过结构哈希进行缓存 解析的表达式，而不是源字符串。与相对复制粘贴 引用产生不同的字符串但相同的 AST 形状，这 在金融模型风格的工作簿中经常出现平行列 股权结构

还值得一看：Microsot 中的“сalculation chain”文档 OOXML SpreadsheetML 规范描述了它们如何序列化 dep 订单 在 xlsx 文件中。不同的问题（持久性与运行时）但是 数据模型为最终的粒度级别提供了信息 务实

Range as first-class is the right priority. Pattern that works: keep ranges as single AST nodes (one dep edge per range, not N), then use interval trees on the reverse side so a cell change at C5 becomes "find intervals covering (C, 5)" instead of scanning all formulas. Pairs well with column-oriented storage if you go there

On the AST caching point, worth caching by structural hash of the parsed expression, not the source string. Copy paste with relative references produces different strings but identical AST shape, which hits a lot in financial-model-style workbooks where parallel columns share structure

Also worth a look: the "сalculation chain" docs in Microsot's OOXML SpreadsheetML spec describe how they serialize the dep order in xlsx files. Different problem (persistence vs runtime) but the data model is informative for what level of granularity ends up being practical

在 HN 中查看
slu 2026/04/27 22:02
好的。我一直在使用 https://github.com/andmarti1424/sc-im 有一段时间了。如果有比较的话就太好了。
Nice. I've been using https://github.com/andmarti1424/sc-im for a while now. It would be great with a comparison.
在 HN 中查看
garritfra 2026/04/27 22:24
Cell 深受这个项目的启发。 FWIW，我在自述文件中添加了一个比较表： https://github.com/garritfra/cell#comparison-with-sc-im
Cell is heavily inspired by this project. FWIW, I added a comparison chart to the README: https://github.com/garritfra/cell#comparison-with-sc-im
在 HN 中查看
k2enemy 2026/04/28 02:50
看起来棒极了！ 当您继续添加功能并考虑脚本编写时，我有一个建议。 sc-im 有 lua 外部函数，但不能传递一系列单元格，只能传递一个单元格的值。 这严重限制了外部lua脚本的用处。 如果/当您添加脚本时，希望您可以克服此限制。 如果是这样，您将至少多一个用户！
Looks terrific! I have a suggestion as you continue to add features and think about scripting. sc-im has lua external functions, but you cannot pass a range of cells, only the value of one cell. This severely limits the usefulness of external lua scripts. If/when you add scripting, hopefully you can overcome this limitation. If so, you'll have at least one more user!
在 HN 中查看
garritfra 2026/04/28 04:03
我看到了它的价值，但我不确定我是否有足够的信心添加适当的脚本，因为这需要大量关注稳定的 API。

尽管如此，我认为在路线图上进行有趣的讨论： https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/23 （AI草拟的RFC）

谢谢！

I see the value, but I'm not sure if I'd be confident enough to add proper scripting yet, as this requires a lot of attention towards a stable API.

Nonetheless, I think it's an intersting discussion to put on the roadmap: https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/23 (RFC sketched out by AI)

Thanks!

daft_pink 2026/04/28 01:59
电子表格如何在终端中开始并绕了一圈又回到终端，真是令人惊奇。

太棒了，感谢您的工作

It’s really amazing how spreadsheet started in the terminal and have come full circle back to the terminal.

This is fantastic thanks for your work

pca2 2026/04/28 02:04
太酷了，我今天就想要一个这样的工具。我希望看到一个小功能请求：支持自定义分隔符。我有时使用实际上使用管道 (|) 作为分隔符的 CSV，以避免包含逗号作为内容的单元格出现问题。
Very cool, I was just wishing for a tool like this today. A small feature request I would love to see: Support for custom delimiters. I sometimes work with CSVs that actually use pipe (|) as the delimiter to avoid issues with cells that contain commas as content.

iammrpayments 2026/04/28 02:23

WillAdams 2026/04/27 21:21
最初希望这不会有 A..B..C... 和 1..2..3... 对于列和行，而是有一个创建类别的 Javelin/Lotus Improv/Quantrix/Flexisheet --- 给我这个，并有一个用于显示公式窗格的动态系统，并且会有一个非常好的工具。
Was initially hopeful that this would rather than have A..B..C... and 1..2..3... for columns and rows would instead have one creating categories à la Javelin/Lotus Improv/Quantrix/Flexisheet --- give me that, and have a dynamic system for displaying a pane of formulae and one would have a _very_ nice tool.
在 HN 中查看
adilkhanovkz 2026/04/28 15:29
尺寸超过 A1/B2 是正确的选择。 Rust 是引擎的良好基础——维度模型中的公式传播需要快速。好奇更大的挑战是引擎还是UI层
Dimensions over A1/B2 is the right move. Rust is a good base for the engine — formula propagation in dimensional models needs to be fast. Curious whether the bigger challenge is the engine or the UI layer
在 HN 中查看
garritfra 2026/04/27 21:58
呵呵，我还没想好，不过我喜欢。我打开了一个问题来跟踪这个问题： https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/11

我必须研究你提到的工具才能真正理解所有细节。谢谢！

Huh, I haven't thought about that yet, but I like it. I opened an issue to track this: https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/11

I'd have to look into the tools you mentioned to really understand all the details. Thanks!

在 HN 中查看
WillAdams 2026/04/27 22:26
谢谢！我很高兴！

我对这个问题发表了评论，希望能够告知这一点 --- 我会注意到，虽然表面上这是一个好主意，但用户界面将非常具有挑战性，并且无法解决它可能会使其不值得追求。

或者，也许正确的方法是某种混合方法，其中可以命名列/行，然后使用所述名称进行引用......

我真的很怀念 Lotus Improv，买不起 Quanttrix，也没有复活 Flexisheet 的编程能力，所以我一直在纠缠人们关于这个问题，但与此同时，我使用 pyspread......

Thanks! I'm gratified!

I made a comment on that issue which hopefully will inform this --- I will note that while on the surface it's a great idea, the UI will be _very_ challenging and not being able to address it may make it not worth pursuing.

Alternately, maybe the correct approach would be some sort of hybrid where columns/rows can be named and then referred to using said names....

I really miss Lotus Improv, and can't afford Quantrix, and don't have the programming chops to resurrect Flexisheet, so I keep pestering folks about this, but in the meanwhile, I use pyspread....

在 HN 中查看
ximm 2026/04/27 22:36
我不久前正在做类似的事情（https://github.com/xi/spreadsheet）但最终没有使用它。当我伸手去拿电子表格时，我并不是第一个想到的工具。不知道为什么。

我一定会尝试你的工具并检查它是否更适合我。

两个随意的想法：

- 在Excel中，我经常使用拖动功能，即使用$A2 + B$1这样的公式，然后向两个方向拖动它。你的工具有类似的东西吗？

- 另一个不错的功能是可视化。在我的工具中，我添加了一个 bar() 函数，它将 0 到 1 之间的值呈现为单元格中的条形。不确定这有多灵活，但我确实从中得到了一些乐趣。

I was working on something similar a while back (https://github.com/xi/spreadsheet) but ended up not using it. I just didn't end up being the first tool that came to mind when I was reaching for a spreadsheet. Not sure why.

I will definitely try out your tool and check if it works better to me.

Two random thoughts:

- In excel I often use the dragging feature, i.e. use a formula like $A2 + B$1 and then drag it in both directions. Does your tool have something similar?

- Another nice feature are visualizations. In my tool I added a bar() function that renders a value between 0 and 1 as a bar in the cell. Not sure how flexible that is, but I did have some fun with it.

在 HN 中查看
garritfra 2026/04/27 22:42
感谢您的尝试，电子表格存储库是很棒的现有技术 - 我将深入研究它。

拖动填充。还没有，但零件大部分都在那里。公式层已经通过分词→AST→eval携带了abs_col/abs_row，所以今天$A2 + B$1解析正确；缺少的是在一定范围内复制公式并仅移动相关组件的编辑操作。为它打开了#17。棘手的部分不是重写，而是键绑定 - Vim 没有明显的“拖动”习惯用法，所以我倾向于视觉选择 + 从锚点填充键（Y 是候选键）或 :fill 命令。如果您对模态编辑器的工作原理有所了解，请接受建议。它还需要落在 #8/#9 中的批量撤消工作之上，因此填充是一个撤消步骤，而不是 N。

bar() / 内联可视化。喜欢它，打开#18。有趣的设计调用是 BAR 是否返回渲染器分派的 CellValue::Visual { … } （在列调整大小下正确，但会影响到 CSV 导出、复制/粘贴以及 SUM 如何处理可视单元格），或者它是否仅在 eval 时返回一串块绘制字符（添加起来很简单，但宽度在计算时被烘焙，这是错误的）。第一个是正确答案；第二个是诱人的MVP。一旦抽象确定下来，SPARKLINE(range) 就是自然的后续行动。

＃17 — https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/17 ＃18 — https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/18

Thanks for trying it, and the spreadsheet repo is great prior art — I'll dig through it.

Drag-fill. Not yet, but the parts are mostly there. The formula layer already carries abs_col/abs_row through tokenization → AST → eval, so $A2 + B$1 parses correctly today; what's missing is the editing op that copies a formula across a range and shifts only the relative components. Opened #17 for it. The tricky part isn't the rewrite, it's the keybinding — Vim doesn't have an obvious idiom for "drag," so I'm leaning toward a visual-selection + fill-from-anchor key (Y is a candidate) or a :fill command. Open to suggestions if you have a feel for what works in a modal editor. It also needs to land on top of the bulk-undo work in #8/#9 so a fill is one undo step instead of N.

bar() / inline visualizations. Love it, opened #18. The interesting design call is whether BAR returns a CellValue::Visual { … } that the renderer dispatches on (correct under column resizes, but ripples into CSV export, copy/paste, and how SUM treats a visual cell), or whether it just returns a string of block-drawing chars at eval time (trivial to add, but width gets baked in at compute time which is wrong). The first is the right answer; the second is a tempting MVP. SPARKLINE(range) is the natural follow-up once the abstraction settles.

#17 — https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/17 #18 — https://github.com/garritfra/cell/issues/18

在 HN 中查看
teo_zero 2026/04/28 05:28
> Vim 没有明显的“拖动”习惯用法，所以我倾向于视觉选择 + 从锚点填充键（Y 是候选键）

更好的 I 或 A，模仿 vim 在块选择后的多重插入。

> Vim doesn't have an obvious idiom for "drag," so I'm leaning toward a visual-selection + fill-from-anchor key (Y is a candidate)

Better I or A, to mimick vim's multiple insert after a block selection.

Theaetetus 2026/04/27 21:56
这太棒了。我经常想要一个低延迟、最小感觉的选项来处理这类事情。 （这些天我越来越少地使用我的 Vim 肌肉记忆！）

真的，感谢您制作和分享这个；到目前为止，我在使用它时感到平静和快乐。

This is great. I often want a low-latency, minimal-feeling option for this sort of thing. (And I get to use my Vim muscle memory less and less often these days!)

Really, thanks for making and sharing this; so far, I feel calm and happy when I'm using it.

jzer0cool 2026/04/27 23:14

Looks great something I want to check out. For comparison anyone share some built-in tools to pretty print-only the csv? Usually what I need to start but formatting is quite off.
在 HN 中查看
bewuethr 2026/04/28 02:37
csvkit（https://github.com/wireservice/csvkit) 有一个“csvlook”命令，并且 yq (https://github.com/mikefarah/yq) 支持 CSV 作为输入格式。
csvkit (https://github.com/wireservice/csvkit) has a "csvlook" command, and yq (https://github.com/mikefarah/yq) supports CSV as an input format.

teo_zero 2026/04/28 05:38

The equal operator should work on strings, too. It would allow formulas like IF(A1=C3,A2,0)

immccc 2026/04/28 03:54
那种东西一旦你看到它，你就会想“我怎么没想到这个主意呢？” :)

唯一的问题是，如果将它作为 vim 插件就好了，这样在打开 .csv 文件或类似文件后，您就可以使用电子表格工具。

The kind of stuff that once you see it, you think "how didn't come the idea up to me?" :)

Only thing is that it would be great to have it as a vim plugin, so that after opening a .csv file or similar, you can make use of the spreadsheet tooling.

unlikelyusernam 2026/04/27 23:39
如果我遗漏了一些明显的东西，请原谅我，但是有计划添加跳跃到特定的行吗？通常，当我滚动并找到我要查找的行时，我只会在那里 nG （其中 n 是我找到的行），在 vi 式编辑器中，它会跳转到该特定行。
forgive me if I'm missing something obvious, but is there any plan to add jumping to a specific row? Usually when I scroll and find the row I'm looking for, I'll just nG there (where n is the row I found), which in vi-esque editors will jump to that specific line.
